revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site | name = Tanushimaru Kofun Cluster | native_name =田主丸古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Fukuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Tanushimaru Kofun Cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|33|19|32|N|130|42|42|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Kurume|Kurume]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = [[Kofun]] cluster | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes ={{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|19|32|N|130|42|42|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Tanushimaru Kofun Cluster'''|田主丸古墳群|Tanushimaru kofun-gun}} is a group of [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofuns]], located in the [[:en:Tanushimaru, Fukuoka|Tanushimaru]] neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Kurume|Kurume]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus cluster was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1961.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/217649|title=田主丸古墳群 / 田主丸大塚古墳 / 寺徳古墳 / 中原狐塚古墳 / 西館古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Tanushimaru Kofun Cluster a general term for the Kofuns in the former Tanushimaru town, and is located on a plateau at the northern foot of the Minou Mountains overlooking the Chikugo Plain. The National Historic Site designation covers four ''kofun''.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
;Tanushimaru Ōtsuka Kofun The {{nihongo|Tanushimaru Ōtsuka Kofun|主丸大塚古墳}} is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It has a total length of 103 meters, and the posterior circular portion has a diameter of 60 meters, making it one of the largest in northern Kyushu. It is orientated to the south. The surface of the mound has ''[[fukiishi]]'', but no ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' have been found. Most of the anterior portion of the mound has been leveled by land clearing and it was previously thought to have been an {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style circular tumulus. It has been [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] several times, starting in the late [[:en:Edo Period|Edo Period]]. The [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is a horizontal-entry stone chamber, but the details are not clear as the interior has not been excavated. Although there is a high possibility that the burial chamber was not subject to major [[:en:Grave robbery|Grave robbery]] and is also a [[decorated kofun]], the investigation of the interior was handed over to future generations, and it was maintained in its current state. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
This tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the late Kofun period, around the latter half of the 6th century, as afterthat time, the construction of keyhole-shaped tumuli came to a halt due to regulations following the [[:en:Iwai Rebellion|Iwai Rebellion]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Jitoku Kofun The {{nihongo|Jitoku Kofun|寺徳古墳}} is an {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style circular tumulus located at the lowest elevation among the Tanushimaru cluster. It was built in the latter half of the 6th century and has a multi-chambered stone burial chamber that opens to the west. The diameter of the mound is 18 meters and the height is approximately three meters. It is a [[decorated kofun]], and on the back and side walls of the burial chamber, triangular patterns, shields, ships, etc. are drawn around concentric circles, and the concentric circles on the back wall are especially boldly drawn in red and green. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Nakabaru Kitsunezuka Kofun The tumulus of the {{nihongo|Nakabaru Kitsunezuka Kofun|中原狐塚古墳}} has mostly been washed away, but it was originally an ''enpun''-style circular tomb with a diameter of 19 meters.The inside of the double-chambered stone burial chamber had decorative murals. Grave goods included bow fittings and iron arrowheads. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Nishidate Kofun The {{nihongo|Nishinodate Kofun|西館古墳}} has an oval shape with a long axis of 14 meters and a short axis of 10.4 meters, and there are also decorative paintings inside the multi-chambered stone burial chamber. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
Currently, the site is open to the public as Ōtsuka Kofun Historical Park; however, the tumuli are sealed to protect the decorative paintings inside.
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)]] *[[Decorated kofun]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.crossroadfukuoka.jp/spot/14854 Fukuoka Tourism Web]{{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.tanushimaru.net/kodaishi Tanushimaru home page] {{in lang|ja}} Tanushimaru Kofun Cluster

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Tanushimaru Kofun Cluster]] {{Infobox ancient site | name = Tanushimaru Kofun Cluster | native_name =田主丸古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Fukuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Tanushimaru Kofun Cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|33|19|32|N|130|42|42|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Kurume|Kurume]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = [[Kofun]] cluster | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes ={{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|19|32|N|130|42|42|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Tanushimaru Kofun Cluster'''|田主丸古墳群|Tanushimaru kofun-gun}} is a group of [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofuns]], located in the [[:en:Tanushimaru, Fukuoka|Tanushimaru]] neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Kurume|Kurume]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus cluster was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1961.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/217649|title=田主丸古墳群 / 田主丸大塚古墳 / 寺徳古墳 / 中原狐塚古墳 / 西館古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Tanushimaru Kofun Cluster a general term for the Kofuns in the former Tanushimaru town, and is located on a plateau at the northern foot of the Minou Mountains overlooking the Chikugo Plain. The National Historic Site designation covers four ''kofun''.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
;Tanushimaru Ōtsuka Kofun The {{nihongo|Tanushimaru Ōtsuka Kofun|主丸大塚古墳}} is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It has a total length of 103 meters, and the posterior circular portion has a diameter of 60 meters, making it one of the largest in northern Kyushu. It is orientated to the south. The surface of the mound has ''[[fukiishi]]'', but no ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' have been found. Most of the anterior portion of the mound has been leveled by land clearing and it was previously thought to have been an {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style circular tumulus. It has been [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] several times, starting in the late [[:en:Edo Period|Edo Period]]. The [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is a horizontal-entry stone chamber, but the details are not clear as the interior has not been excavated. Although there is a high possibility that the burial chamber was not subject to major [[:en:Grave robbery|Grave robbery]] and is also a [[decorated kofun]], the investigation of the interior was handed over to future generations, and it was maintained in its current state. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
This tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the late Kofun period, around the latter half of the 6th century, as afterthat time, the construction of keyhole-shaped tumuli came to a halt due to regulations following the [[:en:Iwai Rebellion|Iwai Rebellion]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Jitoku Kofun The {{nihongo|Jitoku Kofun|寺徳古墳}} is an {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style circular tumulus located at the lowest elevation among the Tanushimaru cluster. It was built in the latter half of the 6th century and has a multi-chambered stone burial chamber that opens to the west. The diameter of the mound is 18 meters and the height is approximately three meters. It is a [[decorated kofun]], and on the back and side walls of the burial chamber, triangular patterns, shields, ships, etc. are drawn around concentric circles, and the concentric circles on the back wall are especially boldly drawn in red and green. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Nakabaru Kitsunezuka Kofun The tumulus of the {{nihongo|Nakabaru Kitsunezuka Kofun|中原狐塚古墳}} has mostly been washed away, but it was originally an ''enpun''-style circular tomb with a diameter of 19 meters.The inside of the double-chambered stone burial chamber had decorative murals. Grave goods included bow fittings and iron arrowheads. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Nishidate Kofun The {{nihongo|Nishinodate Kofun|西館古墳}} has an oval shape with a long axis of 14 meters and a short axis of 10.4 meters, and there are also decorative paintings inside the multi-chambered stone burial chamber. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
Currently, the site is open to the public as Ōtsuka Kofun Historical Park; however, the tumuli are sealed to protect the decorative paintings inside.
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)]] *[[Decorated kofun]]

;Nakabaru Kitsunezuka Kofun The tumulus of the {{nihongo|Nakabaru Kitsunezuka Kofun|中原狐塚古墳}} has mostly been washed away, but it was originally an ''enpun''-style circular tomb with a diameter of 19 meters.The inside of the double-chambered stone burial chamber had decorative murals. Grave goods included bow fittings and iron arrowheads. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Nishidate Kofun The {{nihongo|Nishinodate Kofun|西館古墳}} has an oval shape with a long axis of 14 meters and a short axis of 10.4 meters, and there are also decorative paintings inside the multi-chambered stone burial chamber. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
Currently, the site is open to the public as Ōtsuka Kofun Historical Park; however, the tumuli are sealed to protect the decorative paintings inside.
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)]] *[[Decorated kofun]]

==External links== *[https://www.crossroadfukuoka.jp/spot/14854 Fukuoka Tourism Web]{{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.tanushimaru.net/kodaishi Tanushimaru home page] {{in lang|ja}} Tanushimaru Kofun Cluster

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Tanushimaru Kofun Cluster]] {{Infobox ancient site | name = Tanushimaru Kofun Cluster | native_name =田主丸古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Fukuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Tanushimaru Kofun Cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|33|19|32|N|130|42|42|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Kurume|Kurume]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = [[Kofun]] cluster | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes ={{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|19|32|N|130|42|42|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Tanushimaru Kofun Cluster'''|田主丸古墳群|Tanushimaru kofun-gun}} is a group of [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofuns]], located in the [[:en:Tanushimaru, Fukuoka|Tanushimaru]] neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Kurume|Kurume]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus cluster was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1961.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/217649|title=田主丸古墳群 / 田主丸大塚古墳 / 寺徳古墳 / 中原狐塚古墳 / 西館古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Tanushimaru Kofun Cluster a general term for the Kofuns in the former Tanushimaru town, and is located on a plateau at the northern foot of the Minou Mountains overlooking the Chikugo Plain. The National Historic Site designation covers four ''kofun''.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
;Tanushimaru Ōtsuka Kofun The {{nihongo|Tanushimaru Ōtsuka Kofun|主丸大塚古墳}} is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It has a total length of 103 meters, and the posterior circular portion has a diameter of 60 meters, making it one of the largest in northern Kyushu. It is orientated to the south. The surface of the mound has ''[[fukiishi]]'', but no ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' have been found. Most of the anterior portion of the mound has been leveled by land clearing and it was previously thought to have been an {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style circular tumulus. It has been [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] several times, starting in the late [[:en:Edo Period|Edo Period]]. The [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is a horizontal-entry stone chamber, but the details are not clear as the interior has not been excavated. Although there is a high possibility that the burial chamber was not subject to major [[:en:Grave robbery|Grave robbery]] and is also a [[decorated kofun]], the investigation of the interior was handed over to future generations, and it was maintained in its current state. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
This tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the late Kofun period, around the latter half of the 6th century, as afterthat time, the construction of keyhole-shaped tumuli came to a halt due to regulations following the [[:en:Iwai Rebellion|Iwai Rebellion]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Jitoku Kofun The {{nihongo|Jitoku Kofun|寺徳古墳}} is an {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style circular tumulus located at the lowest elevation among the Tanushimaru cluster. It was built in the latter half of the 6th century and has a multi-chambered stone burial chamber that opens to the west. The diameter of the mound is 18 meters and the height is approximately three meters. It is a [[decorated kofun]], and on the back and side walls of the burial chamber, triangular patterns, shields, ships, etc. are drawn around concentric circles, and the concentric circles on the back wall are especially boldly drawn in red and green. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Nakabaru Kitsunezuka Kofun The tumulus of the {{nihongo|Nakabaru Kitsunezuka Kofun|中原狐塚古墳}} has mostly been washed away, but it was originally an ''enpun''-style circular tomb with a diameter of 19 meters.The inside of the double-chambered stone burial chamber had decorative murals. Grave goods included bow fittings and iron arrowheads. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Nishidate Kofun The {{nihongo|Nishinodate Kofun|西館古墳}} has an oval shape with a long axis of 14 meters and a short axis of 10.4 meters, and there are also decorative paintings inside the multi-chambered stone burial chamber. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
Currently, the site is open to the public as Ōtsuka Kofun Historical Park; however, the tumuli are sealed to protect the decorative paintings inside.
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)]] *[[Decorated kofun]]

Although there is a high possibility that the burial chamber was not subject to major [[:en:Grave robbery|Grave robbery]] and is also a [[decorated kofun]], the investigation of the interior was handed over to future generations, and it was maintained in its current state. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
This tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the late Kofun period, around the latter half of the 6th century, as afterthat time, the construction of keyhole-shaped tumuli came to a halt due to regulations following the [[:en:Iwai Rebellion|Iwai Rebellion]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Jitoku Kofun The {{nihongo|Jitoku Kofun|寺徳古墳}} is an {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style circular tumulus located at the lowest elevation among the Tanushimaru cluster. It was built in the latter half of the 6th century and has a multi-chambered stone burial chamber that opens to the west. The diameter of the mound is 18 meters and the height is approximately three meters. It is a [[decorated kofun]], and on the back and side walls of the burial chamber, triangular patterns, shields, ships, etc. are drawn around concentric circles, and the concentric circles on the back wall are especially boldly drawn in red and green. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Nakabaru Kitsunezuka Kofun The tumulus of the {{nihongo|Nakabaru Kitsunezuka Kofun|中原狐塚古墳}} has mostly been washed away, but it was originally an ''enpun''-style circular tomb with a diameter of 19 meters.The inside of the double-chambered stone burial chamber had decorative murals. Grave goods included bow fittings and iron arrowheads. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Nishidate Kofun The {{nihongo|Nishinodate Kofun|西館古墳}} has an oval shape with a long axis of 14 meters and a short axis of 10.4 meters, and there are also decorative paintings inside the multi-chambered stone burial chamber. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
Currently, the site is open to the public as Ōtsuka Kofun Historical Park; however, the tumuli are sealed to protect the decorative paintings inside.

The {{nihongo|'''Tanushimaru Kofun Cluster'''|田主丸古墳群|Tanushimaru kofun-gun}} is a group of [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofuns]], located in the [[:en:Tanushimaru, Fukuoka|Tanushimaru]] neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Kurume|Kurume]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus cluster was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1961.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/217649|title=田主丸古墳群 / 田主丸大塚古墳 / 寺徳古墳 / 中原狐塚古墳 / 西館古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Tanushimaru Kofun Cluster a general term for the Kofuns in the former Tanushimaru town, and is located on a plateau at the northern foot of the Minou Mountains overlooking the Chikugo Plain. The National Historic Site designation covers four ''kofun''.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
;Tanushimaru Ōtsuka Kofun The {{nihongo|Tanushimaru Ōtsuka Kofun|主丸大塚古墳}} is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It has a total length of 103 meters, and the posterior circular portion has a diameter of 60 meters, making it one of the largest in northern Kyushu. It is orientated to the south. The surface of the mound has ''[[fukiishi]]'', but no ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' have been found. Most of the anterior portion of the mound has been leveled by land clearing and it was previously thought to have been an {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style circular tumulus. It has been [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] several times, starting in the late [[:en:Edo Period|Edo Period]]. The [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is a horizontal-entry stone chamber, but the details are not clear as the interior has not been excavated. Although there is a high possibility that the burial chamber was not subject to major [[:en:Grave robbery|Grave robbery]] and is also a [[decorated kofun]], the investigation of the interior was handed over to future generations, and it was maintained in its current state. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
This tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the late Kofun period, around the latter half of the 6th century, as afterthat time, the construction of keyhole-shaped tumuli came to a halt due to regulations following the [[:en:Iwai Rebellion|Iwai Rebellion]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Jitoku Kofun The {{nihongo|Jitoku Kofun|寺徳古墳}} is an {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style circular tumulus located at the lowest elevation among the Tanushimaru cluster. It was built in the latter half of the 6th century and has a multi-chambered stone burial chamber that opens to the west. The diameter of the mound is 18 meters and the height is approximately three meters. It is a [[decorated kofun]], and on the back and side walls of the burial chamber, triangular patterns, shields, ships, etc. are drawn around concentric circles, and the concentric circles on the back wall are especially boldly drawn in red and green. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Nakabaru Kitsunezuka Kofun The tumulus of the {{nihongo|Nakabaru Kitsunezuka Kofun|中原狐塚古墳}} has mostly been washed away, but it was originally an ''enpun''-style circular tomb with a diameter of 19 meters.The inside of the double-chambered stone burial chamber had decorative murals. Grave goods included bow fittings and iron arrowheads. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Nishidate Kofun The {{nihongo|Nishinodate Kofun|西館古墳}} has an oval shape with a long axis of 14 meters and a short axis of 10.4 meters, and there are also decorative paintings inside the multi-chambered stone burial chamber. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
Currently, the site is open to the public as Ōtsuka Kofun Historical Park; however, the tumuli are sealed to protect the decorative paintings inside.

==Overview== The Tanushimaru Kofun Cluster a general term for the Kofuns in the former Tanushimaru town, and is located on a plateau at the northern foot of the Minou Mountains overlooking the Chikugo Plain. The National Historic Site designation covers four ''kofun''.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
;Tanushimaru Ōtsuka Kofun The {{nihongo|Tanushimaru Ōtsuka Kofun|主丸大塚古墳}} is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It has a total length of 103 meters, and the posterior circular portion has a diameter of 60 meters, making it one of the largest in northern Kyushu. It is orientated to the south. The surface of the mound has ''[[fukiishi]]'', but no ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' have been found. Most of the anterior portion of the mound has been leveled by land clearing and it was previously thought to have been an {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style circular tumulus. It has been [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] several times, starting in the late [[:en:Edo Period|Edo Period]]. The [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is a horizontal-entry stone chamber, but the details are not clear as the interior has not been excavated. Although there is a high possibility that the burial chamber was not subject to major [[:en:Grave robbery|Grave robbery]] and is also a [[decorated kofun]], the investigation of the interior was handed over to future generations, and it was maintained in its current state. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
This tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the late Kofun period, around the latter half of the 6th century, as afterthat time, the construction of keyhole-shaped tumuli came to a halt due to regulations following the [[:en:Iwai Rebellion|Iwai Rebellion]]. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Jitoku Kofun The {{nihongo|Jitoku Kofun|寺徳古墳}} is an {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style circular tumulus located at the lowest elevation among the Tanushimaru cluster. It was built in the latter half of the 6th century and has a multi-chambered stone burial chamber that opens to the west. The diameter of the mound is 18 meters and the height is approximately three meters. It is a [[decorated kofun]], and on the back and side walls of the burial chamber, triangular patterns, shields, ships, etc. are drawn around concentric circles, and the concentric circles on the back wall are especially boldly drawn in red and green. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Nakabaru Kitsunezuka Kofun The tumulus of the {{nihongo|Nakabaru Kitsunezuka Kofun|中原狐塚古墳}} has mostly been washed away, but it was originally an ''enpun''-style circular tomb with a diameter of 19 meters.The inside of the double-chambered stone burial chamber had decorative murals. Grave goods included bow fittings and iron arrowheads. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Nishidate Kofun The {{nihongo|Nishinodate Kofun|西館古墳}} has an oval shape with a long axis of 14 meters and a short axis of 10.4 meters, and there are also decorative paintings inside the multi-chambered stone burial chamber. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
Currently, the site is open to the public as Ōtsuka Kofun Historical Park; however, the tumuli are sealed to protect the decorative paintings inside.
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)]] *[[Decorated kofun]]

The {{nihongo|'''Tanushimaru Kofun Cluster'''|田主丸古墳群|Tanushimaru kofun-gun}} is a group of [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofuns]], located in the {{ill|Tanushimaru, Fukuoka|en|Tanushimaru, Fukuoka|WD=Q7683922|lt=Tanushimaru}} neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Kurume|en|Kurume|WD=Q320934|lt=Kurume}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|WD=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture}} {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. The tumulus cluster was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1961.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/217649|title=田主丸古墳群 / 田主丸大塚古墳 / 寺徳古墳 / 中原狐塚古墳 / 西館古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Tanushimaru Kofun Cluster a general term for the Kofuns in the former Tanushimaru town, and is located on a plateau at the northern foot of the Minou Mountains overlooking the Chikugo Plain. The National Historic Site designation covers four ''kofun''.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
;Tanushimaru Ōtsuka Kofun The {{nihongo|Tanushimaru Ōtsuka Kofun|主丸大塚古墳}} is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It has a total length of 103 meters, and the posterior circular portion has a diameter of 60 meters, making it one of the largest in northern Kyushu. It is orientated to the south. The surface of the mound has ''[[fukiishi]]'', but no ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}}'' have been found. Most of the anterior portion of the mound has been leveled by land clearing and it was previously thought to have been an {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style circular tumulus. It has been [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] several times, starting in the late [[:en:Edo Period|Edo Period]]. The [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is a horizontal-entry stone chamber, but the details are not clear as the interior has not been excavated. Although there is a high possibility that the burial chamber was not subject to major {{ill|Grave robbery|en|Grave robbery|WD=Q1537376|lt=Grave robbery}} and is also a [[decorated kofun]], the investigation of the interior was handed over to future generations, and it was maintained in its current state. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
This tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the late Kofun period, around the latter half of the 6th century, as afterthat time, the construction of keyhole-shaped tumuli came to a halt due to regulations following the {{ill|Iwai Rebellion|en|Iwai Rebellion|WD=Q3138004|lt=Iwai Rebellion}}. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Jitoku Kofun The {{nihongo|Jitoku Kofun|寺徳古墳}} is an {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style circular tumulus located at the lowest elevation among the Tanushimaru cluster. It was built in the latter half of the 6th century and has a multi-chambered stone burial chamber that opens to the west. The diameter of the mound is 18 meters and the height is approximately three meters. It is a [[decorated kofun]], and on the back and side walls of the burial chamber, triangular patterns, shields, ships, etc. are drawn around concentric circles, and the concentric circles on the back wall are especially boldly drawn in red and green. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Nakabaru Kitsunezuka Kofun The tumulus of the {{nihongo|Nakabaru Kitsunezuka Kofun|中原狐塚古墳}} has mostly been washed away, but it was originally an ''enpun''-style circular tomb with a diameter of 19 meters.The inside of the double-chambered stone burial chamber had decorative murals. Grave goods included bow fittings and iron arrowheads. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Nishidate Kofun The {{nihongo|Nishinodate Kofun|西館古墳}} has an oval shape with a long axis of 14 meters and a short axis of 10.4 meters, and there are also decorative paintings inside the multi-chambered stone burial chamber. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
Currently, the site is open to the public as Ōtsuka Kofun Historical Park; however, the tumuli are sealed to protect the decorative paintings inside.
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)|WD=Q13641288|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)}} *[[Decorated kofun]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.crossroadfukuoka.jp/spot/14854 Fukuoka Tourism Web]{{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.tanushimaru.net/kodaishi Tanushimaru home page] {{in lang|ja}} Tanushimaru Kofun Cluster
{{Kofun navbar}}

Although there is a high possibility that the burial chamber was not subject to major {{ill|Grave robbery|en|Grave robbery|WD=Q1537376|lt=Grave robbery}} and is also a [[decorated kofun]], the investigation of the interior was handed over to future generations, and it was maintained in its current state. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
This tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the late Kofun period, around the latter half of the 6th century, as afterthat time, the construction of keyhole-shaped tumuli came to a halt due to regulations following the {{ill|Iwai Rebellion|en|Iwai Rebellion|WD=Q3138004|lt=Iwai Rebellion}}. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Jitoku Kofun The {{nihongo|Jitoku Kofun|寺徳古墳}} is an {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style circular tumulus located at the lowest elevation among the Tanushimaru cluster. It was built in the latter half of the 6th century and has a multi-chambered stone burial chamber that opens to the west. The diameter of the mound is 18 meters and the height is approximately three meters. It is a [[decorated kofun]], and on the back and side walls of the burial chamber, triangular patterns, shields, ships, etc. are drawn around concentric circles, and the concentric circles on the back wall are especially boldly drawn in red and green. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Nakabaru Kitsunezuka Kofun The tumulus of the {{nihongo|Nakabaru Kitsunezuka Kofun|中原狐塚古墳}} has mostly been washed away, but it was originally an ''enpun''-style circular tomb with a diameter of 19 meters.The inside of the double-chambered stone burial chamber had decorative murals. Grave goods included bow fittings and iron arrowheads. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Nishidate Kofun The {{nihongo|Nishinodate Kofun|西館古墳}} has an oval shape with a long axis of 14 meters and a short axis of 10.4 meters, and there are also decorative paintings inside the multi-chambered stone burial chamber. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
Currently, the site is open to the public as Ōtsuka Kofun Historical Park; however, the tumuli are sealed to protect the decorative paintings inside.
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)|WD=Q13641288|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)}} *[[Decorated kofun]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Tanushimaru Kofun Cluster]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Tanushimaru Kofun Cluster]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Tanushimaru Kofun Cluster]] {{Infobox ancient site | name = Tanushimaru Kofun Cluster | native_name =田主丸古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Fukuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Tanushimaru Kofun Cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|33|19|32|N|130|42|42|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Kurume|en|Kurume|WD=Q320934|lt=Kurume}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}} | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = [[Kofun]] cluster | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes ={{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|19|32|N|130|42|42|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Tanushimaru Kofun Cluster'''|田主丸古墳群|Tanushimaru kofun-gun}} is a group of [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofuns]], located in the {{ill|Tanushimaru, Fukuoka|en|Tanushimaru, Fukuoka|WD=Q7683922|lt=Tanushimaru}} neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Kurume|en|Kurume|WD=Q320934|lt=Kurume}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|WD=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture}} {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. The tumulus cluster was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1961.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/217649|title=田主丸古墳群 / 田主丸大塚古墳 / 寺徳古墳 / 中原狐塚古墳 / 西館古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Tanushimaru Kofun Cluster a general term for the Kofuns in the former Tanushimaru town, and is located on a plateau at the northern foot of the Minou Mountains overlooking the Chikugo Plain. The National Historic Site designation covers four ''kofun''.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
;Tanushimaru Ōtsuka Kofun The {{nihongo|Tanushimaru Ōtsuka Kofun|主丸大塚古墳}} is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It has a total length of 103 meters, and the posterior circular portion has a diameter of 60 meters, making it one of the largest in northern Kyushu. It is orientated to the south. The surface of the mound has ''[[fukiishi]]'', but no ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}}'' have been found. Most of the anterior portion of the mound has been leveled by land clearing and it was previously thought to have been an {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style circular tumulus. It has been [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] several times, starting in the late [[:en:Edo Period|Edo Period]]. The [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is a horizontal-entry stone chamber, but the details are not clear as the interior has not been excavated. Although there is a high possibility that the burial chamber was not subject to major {{ill|Grave robbery|en|Grave robbery|WD=Q1537376|lt=Grave robbery}} and is also a [[decorated kofun]], the investigation of the interior was handed over to future generations, and it was maintained in its current state. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
This tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the late Kofun period, around the latter half of the 6th century, as afterthat time, the construction of keyhole-shaped tumuli came to a halt due to regulations following the {{ill|Iwai Rebellion|en|Iwai Rebellion|WD=Q3138004|lt=Iwai Rebellion}}. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Jitoku Kofun The {{nihongo|Jitoku Kofun|寺徳古墳}} is an {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style circular tumulus located at the lowest elevation among the Tanushimaru cluster. It was built in the latter half of the 6th century and has a multi-chambered stone burial chamber that opens to the west. The diameter of the mound is 18 meters and the height is approximately three meters. It is a [[decorated kofun]], and on the back and side walls of the burial chamber, triangular patterns, shields, ships, etc. are drawn around concentric circles, and the concentric circles on the back wall are especially boldly drawn in red and green. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Nakabaru Kitsunezuka Kofun The tumulus of the {{nihongo|Nakabaru Kitsunezuka Kofun|中原狐塚古墳}} has mostly been washed away, but it was originally an ''enpun''-style circular tomb with a diameter of 19 meters.The inside of the double-chambered stone burial chamber had decorative murals. Grave goods included bow fittings and iron arrowheads. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Nishidate Kofun The {{nihongo|Nishinodate Kofun|西館古墳}} has an oval shape with a long axis of 14 meters and a short axis of 10.4 meters, and there are also decorative paintings inside the multi-chambered stone burial chamber. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
Currently, the site is open to the public as Ōtsuka Kofun Historical Park; however, the tumuli are sealed to protect the decorative paintings inside.
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)|WD=Q13641288|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)}} *[[Decorated kofun]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Tanushimaru Kofun Cluster]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Tanushimaru Kofun Cluster]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Tanushimaru Kofun Cluster]] {{Infobox ancient site | name = Tanushimaru Kofun Cluster | native_name =田主丸古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Fukuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Tanushimaru Kofun Cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|33|19|32|N|130|42|42|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Kurume|en|Kurume|WD=Q320934|lt=Kurume|1=Kurume}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = [[Kofun]] cluster | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes ={{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|19|32|N|130|42|42|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Tanushimaru Kofun Cluster'''|田主丸古墳群|Tanushimaru kofun-gun}} is a group of [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofuns]], located in the {{ill|Tanushimaru, Fukuoka|en|Tanushimaru, Fukuoka|WD=Q7683922|lt=Tanushimaru|1=Tanushimaru, Fukuoka}} neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Kurume|en|Kurume|WD=Q320934|lt=Kurume|1=Kurume}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|WD=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture|1=Fukuoka Prefecture}} {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The tumulus cluster was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1961.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/217649|title=田主丸古墳群 / 田主丸大塚古墳 / 寺徳古墳 / 中原狐塚古墳 / 西館古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Tanushimaru Kofun Cluster a general term for the Kofuns in the former Tanushimaru town, and is located on a plateau at the northern foot of the Minou Mountains overlooking the Chikugo Plain. The National Historic Site designation covers four ''kofun''.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
;Tanushimaru Ōtsuka Kofun The {{nihongo|Tanushimaru Ōtsuka Kofun|主丸大塚古墳}} is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It has a total length of 103 meters, and the posterior circular portion has a diameter of 60 meters, making it one of the largest in northern Kyushu. It is orientated to the south. The surface of the mound has ''[[fukiishi]]'', but no ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'' have been found. Most of the anterior portion of the mound has been leveled by land clearing and it was previously thought to have been an {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style circular tumulus. It has been [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] several times, starting in the late [[:en:Edo Period|Edo Period]]. The [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is a horizontal-entry stone chamber, but the details are not clear as the interior has not been excavated. Although there is a high possibility that the burial chamber was not subject to major {{ill|Grave robbery|en|Grave robbery|WD=Q1537376|lt=Grave robbery|1=Grave robbery}} and is also a [[decorated kofun]], the investigation of the interior was handed over to future generations, and it was maintained in its current state. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
This tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the late Kofun period, around the latter half of the 6th century, as afterthat time, the construction of keyhole-shaped tumuli came to a halt due to regulations following the {{ill|Iwai Rebellion|en|Iwai Rebellion|WD=Q3138004|lt=Iwai Rebellion|1=Iwai Rebellion}}. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Jitoku Kofun The {{nihongo|Jitoku Kofun|寺徳古墳}} is an {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style circular tumulus located at the lowest elevation among the Tanushimaru cluster. It was built in the latter half of the 6th century and has a multi-chambered stone burial chamber that opens to the west. The diameter of the mound is 18 meters and the height is approximately three meters. It is a [[decorated kofun]], and on the back and side walls of the burial chamber, triangular patterns, shields, ships, etc. are drawn around concentric circles, and the concentric circles on the back wall are especially boldly drawn in red and green. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Nakabaru Kitsunezuka Kofun The tumulus of the {{nihongo|Nakabaru Kitsunezuka Kofun|中原狐塚古墳}} has mostly been washed away, but it was originally an ''enpun''-style circular tomb with a diameter of 19 meters.The inside of the double-chambered stone burial chamber had decorative murals. Grave goods included bow fittings and iron arrowheads. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Nishidate Kofun The {{nihongo|Nishinodate Kofun|西館古墳}} has an oval shape with a long axis of 14 meters and a short axis of 10.4 meters, and there are also decorative paintings inside the multi-chambered stone burial chamber. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
Currently, the site is open to the public as Ōtsuka Kofun Historical Park; however, the tumuli are sealed to protect the decorative paintings inside.
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)|WD=Q13641288|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)}} *[[Decorated kofun]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://www.crossroadfukuoka.jp/spot/14854 Fukuoka Tourism Web]{{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.tanushimaru.net/kodaishi Tanushimaru home page] {{in lang|ja}} Tanushimaru Kofun Cluster
{{Kofun navbar}}

==Overview== The Tanushimaru Kofun Cluster a general term for the Kofuns in the former Tanushimaru town, and is located on a plateau at the northern foot of the Minou Mountains overlooking the Chikugo Plain. The National Historic Site designation covers four ''kofun''.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
;Tanushimaru Ōtsuka Kofun The {{nihongo|Tanushimaru Ōtsuka Kofun|主丸大塚古墳}} is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpō-kōen-fun|1=Zenpō-kōen-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpō-kōen-fun|1=[[Zenpō-kōen-fun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It has a total length of 103 meters, and the posterior circular portion has a diameter of 60 meters, making it one of the largest in northern Kyushu. It is orientated to the south. The surface of the mound has ''[[fukiishi]]'', but no ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'' have been found. Most of the anterior portion of the mound has been leveled by land clearing and it was previously thought to have been an {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style circular tumulus. It has been [[archaeological excavation|excavated]] several times, starting in the late [[:en:Edo Period|Edo Period]]. The [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] is a horizontal-entry stone chamber, but the details are not clear as the interior has not been excavated. Although there is a high possibility that the burial chamber was not subject to major {{ill|Grave robbery|en|Grave robbery|WD=Q1537376|lt=Grave robbery|1=Grave robbery}} and is also a [[decorated kofun]], the investigation of the interior was handed over to future generations, and it was maintained in its current state. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
This tumulus is estimated to have been constructed in the late Kofun period, around the latter half of the 6th century, as afterthat time, the construction of keyhole-shaped tumuli came to a halt due to regulations following the {{ill|Iwai Rebellion|en|Iwai Rebellion|WD=Q3138004|lt=Iwai Rebellion|1=Iwai Rebellion}}. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Jitoku Kofun The {{nihongo|Jitoku Kofun|寺徳古墳}} is an {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}}-style circular tumulus located at the lowest elevation among the Tanushimaru cluster. It was built in the latter half of the 6th century and has a multi-chambered stone burial chamber that opens to the west. The diameter of the mound is 18 meters and the height is approximately three meters. It is a [[decorated kofun]], and on the back and side walls of the burial chamber, triangular patterns, shields, ships, etc. are drawn around concentric circles, and the concentric circles on the back wall are especially boldly drawn in red and green. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Nakabaru Kitsunezuka Kofun The tumulus of the {{nihongo|Nakabaru Kitsunezuka Kofun|中原狐塚古墳}} has mostly been washed away, but it was originally an ''enpun''-style circular tomb with a diameter of 19 meters.The inside of the double-chambered stone burial chamber had decorative murals. Grave goods included bow fittings and iron arrowheads. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
;Nishidate Kofun The {{nihongo|Nishinodate Kofun|西館古墳}} has an oval shape with a long axis of 14 meters and a short axis of 10.4 meters, and there are also decorative paintings inside the multi-chambered stone burial chamber. <ref name="Shiseki"/>
Currently, the site is open to the public as Ōtsuka Kofun Historical Park; however, the tumuli are sealed to protect the decorative paintings inside.
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)|WD=Q13641288|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Fukuoka)}} *[[Decorated kofun]]

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Tanushimaru Kofun Cluster | native_name =田主丸古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = | image_size = | alt = | caption = | map_type = Japan Fukuoka Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Tanushimaru Kofun Cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|33|19|32|N|130|42|42|E|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Kurume|en|Kurume|WD=Q320934|lt=Kurume|1=Kurume}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = [[Kofun]] cluster | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes ={{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|33|19|32|N|130|42|42|E}}}}}}
The {{nihongo|'''Tanushimaru Kofun Cluster'''|田主丸古墳群|Tanushimaru kofun-gun}} is a group of [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofuns]], located in the {{ill|Tanushimaru, Fukuoka|en|Tanushimaru, Fukuoka|WD=Q7683922|lt=Tanushimaru|1=Tanushimaru, Fukuoka}} neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Kurume|en|Kurume|WD=Q320934|lt=Kurume|1=Kurume}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|WD=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture|1=Fukuoka Prefecture}} {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The tumulus cluster was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1961.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/217649|title=田主丸古墳群 / 田主丸大塚古墳 / 寺徳古墳 / 中原狐塚古墳 / 西館古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=December 20, 2023}}</ref>
